signals.title: NZD/JPY — G10 Bilateral Theme + Geopolitical Risk Premium
signals.text: NZD 1W momentum (+1.390%) faces a sharp reversal risk as the 11% surge in WTI crude and US-Iran escalation drive safe-haven demand into the laggard JPY. The 1.879% G10 scorecard spread is vulnerable to compression as the Nikkei's 4.03% drop signals a massive unwind of carry-funded risk positions. The next BoJ meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 31 Jul remains the primary anchor for JPY normalization against the NZD's accommodative -0.58% real rate.

signals.title: USD/JPY — JPY COT Short Squeeze + Inflationary Oil Shock
signals.text: Fed 3.75%. While the +264bp OIS carry advantage favors USD, the 12.19% spike in VIX and JPY's positive economic surprise index (net +8) support a tactical JPY recovery from Friday's 162.35 close. The next BoJ meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 31 Jul is the catalyst for determining if the yield gap narrows further.

signals.title: USD/CAD — CAD COT Short + Trade Tariff Escalation
signals.text: CAD COT net short -93,515 (79.4% short) reinforces downside pressure on the Loonie following Trump's weekend threats of new Canadian tariffs. Despite the 11% weekly surge in WTI oil typically supporting CAD, the -1.97σ correlation break suggests trade war fears and the +132bp USD carry advantage are the dominant drivers. The next BoC meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) determines if the central bank can maintain its hold against deteriorating trade relations.

signals.title: EUR/USD — ECB Policy Divergence + Data Underperformance
signals.text: EUR COT net short -74,637 aligns with a strong negative economic surprise index (net -16) to keep the bias tilted toward 1.1400. The +144bp OIS carry advantage for USD is reinforced by the US real rate of +1.47% vs the EUR's near-zero +0.03%, limiting upside ahead of the ECB decision. The next ECB meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 23 Jul is the catalyst for confirming the hold bias amidst rising energy-driven inflation risks.

signals.title: EUR/GBP — Retail Sentiment Extreme + Negative CESI Alignment
signals.text: Retail positioning is 88% long EUR/GBP, creating a contrarian signal for downside as the pair's 1.1446 close faces pressure from the Euro's persistent data misses. While the carry spread is narrow, the GBP's mildly restrictive +0.78% real rate offers a superior yield profile compared to the EUR's +0.03%. The next BoE meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 30 Jul serves as the resolution point for this relative value divergence.
